fact: June 12 sits exactly 163 days into the year, which means there are only 202 days left until New Year's Eve!
In the Northern Hemisphere, this date falls smack in the middle of prime firefly season - those magical blinking bugs are having their biggest light shows right now!
Why do strawberries taste extra sweet around June 12? Because this is peak strawberry season in most temperate regions, when the berries are bursting with natural sugars!
June 12 is only 9 days away from the summer solstice, making it one of the longest days of the year in terms of daylight hours for anyone living north of the equator.
Baby deer (called fawns) are stumbling around on wobbly legs right now because June is peak fawn birthing season in North America!
In Antarctica, June 12 falls during the heart of winter, when temperatures can drop to a bone-chilling -40°F and the continent is shrouded in near-total darkness.
Rose petals are at their most fragrant around this date because June marks peak blooming season for most rose varieties - their scent is literally designed to attract pollinators right now!
The sun rises as early as 4:30 AM in northern cities like Seattle on June 12, giving kids almost 16 hours of daylight to play outside!
June 12 falls during Gemini season (May 21 - June 20), which astrologers say makes it a day ruled by curiosity, communication, and twin-like energy.
Ocean water temperatures in the Northern Hemisphere are warming up fast around this date, making it the perfect time for sea turtles to start their nesting season on beaches.
Lightning bugs aren't the only things glowing - certain types of mushrooms called foxfire fungi glow green in the dark during humid June nights!
